questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Basic Profile & Identity
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include basic identity and profile questions as foundation for F1 visa interview

actions.reason: Basic identity verification establishes applicant eligibility and forms foundation for F1 visa assessment including age requirement (18+)

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Study Plans & Motivation
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include study motivation and career alignment questions

actions.reason: Study motivation assessment is critical to determine genuine educational intent vs. immigration intent, core evaluation criteria for F1 visa

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - University Choice & I-20
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include university selection and I-20 documentation questions

actions.reason: University verification confirms I-20 receipt, legitimate admission, and SEVIS compliance, mandatory requirements for F1 visa processing

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Academic Capability
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include test scores and academic preparation questions

actions.reason: Academic capability verification through test scores (TOEFL/IELTS, GRE/GMAT/SAT), GPA, and English proficiency is mandatory for F1 visa approval

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Financial Capability
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include financial sponsor and funding questions - critical for F1

actions.reason: Financial capability verification is critical for F1 visa - officers must confirm applicant/sponsor can fund entire education without US public benefits

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Home Country Ties & Return Intent
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include post-graduation plans and home country ties questions - critical for F1

actions.reason: Return home intent and home country ties are CRITICAL for F1 visa - officers must establish non-immigrant intent and evidence of commitment to return

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong English Proficiency
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has high English test scores (TOEFL 100+ or IELTS 8+)

actions.reason: Strong English proficiency significantly strengthens F1 application by confirming student can succeed in English-medium academic environment

questionSelectionRules.name: Weak English Proficiency
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has low English test scores (TOEFL below 80 or IELTS below 6.5) or low confidence rating

actions.reason: Weak English proficiency raises concerns about academic success and requires detailed explanation of support systems and strategies for managing English-medium coursework

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Academic Record
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has high GPA (3.5+) and strong test scores

actions.reason: Strong academic history demonstrates capability for US university coursework and reduces officer concerns about academic success

questionSelectionRules.name: Weak Academic Record
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has low GPA (below 3.0) or low test scores

actions.reason: Weaker academic record requires detailed explanation of academic preparation strategies and confirmation of financial support for full program duration despite potential academic challenges

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Financial Capability
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when sponsor income is high (100,000+ USD) or student has full scholarship

actions.reason: Strong financial capability and funding sources significantly strengthen F1 visa application by eliminating officer concern about student becoming public charge

questionSelectionRules.name: Weak or Uncertain Financial Capability
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when sponsor income is modest (below 40,000 USD) or available funds barely cover program costs

actions.reason: Limited financial resources require detailed documentation of sponsor income proof, detailed funding plan for all program years, and clear explanation of how expenses will be covered

questionSelectionRules.name: No Scholarship Funding
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student did not receive any scholarship

actions.reason: Absence of scholarship means full reliance on sponsor/personal funds - requires strong evidence of complete financial capability for entire program duration

questionSelectionRules.name: Has Scholarship Funding
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student received any type of scholarship (merit-based, need-based, or partial)

actions.reason: University scholarship significantly strengthens financial capability profile by confirming university investment in student and reducing sponsor burden

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Home Country Ties
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has multiple home country ties: family, property, job prospects, and strong intent to return

actions.reason: Multiple strong home country ties (family, property, job prospects) provide clear evidence of non-immigrant intent and commitment to return, significantly strengthening F1 visa case

questionSelectionRules.name: Weak Home Country Ties
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has few home country ties or uncertain return plans

actions.reason: Limited home country ties or weak return intent raises officer concerns about non-immigrant intent and requires detailed, credible explanation of why student will return despite weak ties

questionSelectionRules.name: Considering USA Stay (OPT/H1B)
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student is considering staying in USA after graduation

actions.reason: OPT/H1B consideration must be carefully framed as temporary work experience followed by return home to maintain F1 non-immigrant intent. Requires clear explanation of why temporary US work aligns with long-term home country plans

questionSelectionRules.name: Has US Relatives
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has relatives living in the USA

actions.reason: US relatives can create immigration concern - officer may suspect student intends to stay with relatives despite return intent. Requires clear emphasis on stronger home country family ties and genuine commitment to return

questionSelectionRules.name: Visa Refusal History
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has been denied a US visa before

actions.reason: Visa refusal history is serious red flag - requires transparent explanation of refusal reason, evidence that circumstances have changed, and demonstration that applicant is now clearly eligible for F1 visa

questionSelectionRules.name: Criminal Record or Legal Issues
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has any criminal convictions or legal issues

actions.reason: Criminal history or visa violations are serious impediments to F1 approval - requires full disclosure, detailed explanation of circumstances, evidence of rehabilitation, and assurance of future lawful conduct

questionSelectionRules.name: Graduate Student (Master's/PhD)
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student is pursuing Master's degree or PhD

actions.reason: Graduate students often have better home country job prospects and credentials, can articulate specific career goals, and present stronger non-immigrant intent

questionSelectionRules.name: Undergraduate Student (Bachelor's)
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student is pursuing Bachelor's degree

actions.reason: Undergraduate students pursuing 4-year programs require careful assessment of return intent since long program duration and young age may raise immigration intent concerns

questionSelectionRules.name: Employed with Work Experience
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student is currently employed with 2+ years work experience

actions.reason: Work experience demonstrates maturity, financial stability, clear professional motivation for studies, and ability to return to meaningful employment post-graduation

questionSelectionRules.name: Not Employed / Recent Graduate
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student is not currently employed

actions.reason: Unemployed status requires clear focus on academic preparation and strong financial backup from sponsor to address concerns about student's financial independence and preparedness

questionSelectionRules.name: Previous US Visit Experience
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has visited USA before

actions.reason: Previous US visits demonstrate clean immigration history, compliance with visa terms, real familiarity with US environment, and opportunity for campus visits which strengthen university commitment

questionSelectionRules.name: No Previous US Visit
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has never visited USA before

actions.reason: No US experience requires extra effort to demonstrate detailed university knowledge through virtual research, shows genuine interest despite not visiting campus, and reinforces commitment to specific university

questionSelectionRules.name: Has I-20 Document
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has received I-20 from university (ready for visa interview)

actions.reason: Possession of I-20 document confirms admission, SEVIS status, and financial certification, significantly advancing interview readiness and demonstrating concrete progress

questionSelectionRules.name: I-20 Not Yet Received
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has not yet received I-20 from university

actions.reason: Missing I-20 document indicates incomplete application process - requires explanation of timeline for I-20 receipt and confirmation that financial documentation is ready for SEVIS certification

questionSelectionRules.name: High-Risk Profile: Weak Academics + Weak Finances + Weak Ties
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has combination of concerning factors: low GPA, low test scores, limited funds, weak home country ties

actions.reason: High-risk combination of weak academics + limited finances + weak home country ties requires comprehensive evidence addressing ALL concerns: strong support plan for academics, complete financial documentation, and very clear credible explanation of return intent despite weak ties

questionSelectionRules.name: Student from Low-Income Country with Resources
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student is from developing country (Pakistan, India, China, etc) with strong financial support

actions.reason: Strong financial resources in developing country context often indicate family business or high-status employment, strengthening both financial capability and home country ties arguments

questionSelectionRules.name: Non-English Medium Education + English Study Needed
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student did not study in English medium previously

actions.reason: Non-English prior education requires detailed explanation of English proficiency development, specific strategies for managing English-medium coursework, and discussion of university support systems

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong University-Field Match
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student's field of study aligns with university's strengths (e.g., CS student at MIT, Business at Wharton)

actions.reason: Strong academic fit between student's field and university's programs demonstrates thoughtful application strategy and genuine educational motivation

questionSelectionRules.name: Multiple University Admissions
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student received multiple admission offers (3+), showing competitive application

actions.reason: Multiple admissions demonstrate strong academic competitiveness, quality of application, and choices available, supporting argument of genuine educational opportunity in USA

questionSelectionRules.name: Single University Admission
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student received only one admission offer

actions.reason: Single admission may require explanation of application strategy and reaffirmation of commitment to this particular university as genuine first-choice institution

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Standardized Test Scores (GRE/GMAT/SAT/ACT)
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student has high standardized test scores (GRE 310+, GMAT 650+, SAT 1400+)

actions.reason: Strong standardized test performance demonstrates academic aptitude in quantitative reasoning and critical thinking, supporting capability for US university-level coursework

questionSelectionRules.name: No Standardized Test (GRE/GMAT/SAT/ACT)
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when student did not take standardized test

actions.reason: Absence of standardized test requires reliance on GPA and TOEFL/IELTS to demonstrate academic capability; may indicate test not required by program but relies more heavily on other credentials
